sanskrit:
निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः।
शारीरं केवलं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम्॥
transliteration:
nirāśīr yata-cittātmā tyakta-sarva-parigrahaḥ |
śārīraṃ kevalaṃ karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣam ||
literal_meaning: One who is free from expectations, whose mind and self are disciplined, who has abandoned all sense of possession, performs action only for bodily sustenance and incurs no sin.
contextual_meaning: When action is stripped of personal agenda and reduced to its minimal, life-sustaining function, the psyche is no longer haunted by guilt or karmic residue; the ego’s claim of ownership is the true source of psychological bondage.
psychological_state: Post-egoic functioning: the mind behaves like a well-oiled machine part, experiencing no self-referential anxiety because the narrative of ‘I am the doer’ has been suspended.
trigger_scenario: You’re asked to work overtime without extra pay; instead of resentment or pride you quietly finish the task, viewing it as mere bodily maintenance, and leave the office without replaying the story in your head.
anchor_text: Work only to keep the body alive, owning nothing, expecting nothing.
